prompt: A cinematic historical scene from Romance of the Three Kingdoms showing the Peach Garden Oath. Three ancient Chinese men stand side by side behind a ceremonial altar table in a blooming orchard of pink peach blossoms, each holding a respectful fist-and-palm salute at chest height. The man on the left is broad and heavily built, wearing dark lamellar armor with a deep red robe panel and rugged warrior styling; the man in the center is shorter and slimmer, wearing layered brown scholar-official robes and a simple topknot; the man on the right is tall and imposing, wearing flowing dark green robes with armor details, a long black beard, and a noble commanding presence. In front of them is one large carved wooden altar table arranged with exactly 10 ritual elements: 1 bronze incense burner in the center with three lit incense sticks and visible curling smoke, 4 bronze candlesticks with small flames placed near the corners, 1 fruit plate with peaches, 1 ewer or wine vessel on the left, 2 bronze pouring vessels on the right, and 1 open scroll laid across the tabletop. Pink petals drift through the air and scatter on the ground. The orchard forms a tunnel of flowering branches overhead, with rustic wooden fencing and traditional buildings softly visible in the background through light spring haze. Include exactly 2 horses flanking the group: a dark horse on the far left and a white horse on the far right, both saddled and calm. Composition is symmetrical, eye-level, centered on the oath ritual, with rich period textures, realistic faces and fabrics, soft natural morning light, shallow atmospheric depth, and an epic, reverent, painterly-cinematic realism in the style of a prestige historical drama.
